stories.title: Hormuz reopens, but insurers aren't ready to sound the all-clear
stories.description: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?

stories.title: Gasoline could drop below $4 in coming days
stories.description: Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: The Strait of Hormuz opens for commercial ships but the U.S. blockade continues on Iran
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.

stories.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
stories.description: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

stories.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.

stories.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
stories.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US
stories.description: Israel strikes hit southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fires rockets at Israel, a day after rare direct negotiations.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.description: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Middle East live: Iran threatens to close Strait of Hormuz again if US blockade continues
stories.description: Tehran on Saturday threatened to cut off access to the Strait of Hormuz again if the United States maintains its blockade of Iranian ports, raising fresh doubts over recent progress following a ceasefire in Lebanon. Follow our live blog for the latest updates.

stories.title: Tale of two truces: Lebanon 'steps up' alongside Israel as Tehran and Washington edge toward a deal
stories.description: Gavin Lee is pleased to welcome Robert B. Murrett, retired Vice Admiral in the US Navy, Professor of Practice and Deputy Director of the Syracuse University's Institute for Security Policy and Law. The Israel-Lebanon and US-Iran ceasefires are at a critical juncture In the evolvi...

stories.title: Trump says Iran peace deal could be reached ‘soon’ after Tehran reopens Hormuz Strait
stories.description: Tehran's announcement on Friday that the critical Strait of Hormuz was reopen to commercial shipping for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was followed by a flurry of social media posts by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a peace deal could be reached "soon"....

stories.title: Iran, Magyar, Mythos & the Pope
stories.description: In a week where Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open for commercial trade—effectively ending its nearly seven-week de facto blockade of the critical shipping lane, President Trump imposed a U.S. blockade on Iranian ports to strangle its ability to trade. Trump vowed to c...

stories.title: Macron, Meloni, Starmer and Merz welcome Hormuz Strait reopening
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announc...

stories.title: 1.2 million people uprooted by war
stories.description: More than a million civilians in Lebanon have been displaced since the war in the Middle East erupted at the end of February. Among them are thousands of young mothers and pregnant women, many forced to give birth in makeshift camps or other inadequate conditions. This report fro...

stories.title: Macron urges permanent reopening of Hormuz Strait
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announc...

stories.title: Europe welcomes Hormuz Strait reopening
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announc...

stories.title: Lebanese man removes Israeli flag from castle in southern Lebanon
stories.description: A Lebanese man who returned to southern Lebanon removes the Israeli flag from Beaufort Castle (Qalaat al-Shaqif).

stories.title: Trump thanks Gulf states for their ‘tremendous’ support
stories.description: ‘This will be a great and brilliant day for the world’ Donald Trump said as Iran fully opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US blockade of ports must end as strait opens
stories.description: Tens of thousands of people forcibly displaced by Israeli attacks on Lebanon use first day of ceasefire to return home.

stories.title: Iran rejects Trump claim on deal to surrender nuclear material stockpiles
stories.description: Iran has rejected suggestions by Donald Trump that it has agreed to hand over its stockpile of enriched uranium.

stories.title: Lebanon president says country is no longer a pawn amid Israel ceasefire
stories.description: President Joseph Aoun promised to work to preserve Lebanon's sovereignty and freedom.

stories.title: Will the ceasefire in Lebanon hold?
stories.description: Lebanon and Israel are holding first direct talks in decades.

stories.title: Displaced family reunited with grandfather in Lebanon’s Tyre
stories.description: A grandfather was reunited with his son and grandchildren in Tyre, southern Lebanon.

stories.title: World reacts to Strait of Hormuz reopening amid US-Iran conflict
stories.description: Politicians and industry welcome reopening of the vital waterway after throttling of traffic caused oil prices to spike.

stories.title: Trump says US blockade of Iranian ports will ‘remain’ if no deal reached
stories.description: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he planned to maintain a US blockade of Iranian ports if a peace deal with Tehran is not reached, adding that he may not extend the ceasefire after its expiration. Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz on Friday in the wake of a ceasefire...

stories.title: More than 38,000 girls, women killed in Gaza conflict, UN agency says
stories.description: GENEVA: Over 38,000 women and girls were killed in Gaza Strip by the end of last year, the UN estimated Friday, amounting to over half of the 71,000 deaths recorded by the territory’s health ministry since Oct 2023. “Between Oct 2023 and Dec 2025, more than 38,000 women and girls...

stories.title: Pakistan escorted Iran negotiators home amid Israel concerns
stories.description: • Sources say Islamabad deployed some two dozen jets for mission• Airborne Warning and Control System also used for aerial surveillance• Similar protection ‘may be offered to Iranian officials in next round of talks if needed’ ISLAMABAD: Pakistan Air Force escorted Iranian negoti...

stories.title: US cloud dominance risks European security: report
stories.description: PARIS: More than three-quarters of European countries use American cloud computing services for vital national security functions, a report published Friday found, warning that the dependency could make governments vulnerable. The analysis arrives as European governments increasi...

stories.title: Pakistan escorted Iranian negotiators home from peace talks amid concerns of Israeli attack, sources say
stories.description: The air force escorted Iranian negotiators home from inconclusive peace talks with the United States last weekend, launching a major operation after the Iranians said Israel might seek to kill them, three sources told Reuters. Pakistan deployed some two dozen jets in the escort, ...

stories.title: Trump hints at Islamabad trip if Iran deal is finalised
stories.description: PRIME Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Qatar’s Emir Sheikh Tamim shake hands in Doha; and (right) Field Marshal Asim Munir meets Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf in Tehran.—Reuters/AFP • Claims Iran has accepted ‘almost everything’ in talks• Nuclear issue remains ke...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Trump says Iran ceasefire may end if no deal reached
stories.description: "Maybe I ​won't ‌extend it, but the blockade is ‌going to ​remain," U.S. President Donald Trump told reporters ⁠on Air Force One on ‌his way back to Washington from Phoenix, Arizona

stories.title: Iran threatens to again close Strait of Hormuz, if U.S. blockade continues
stories.description: Iran, however, pushed back on Trump’s claim that Tehran had agreed to hand over its enriched uranium, saying its stockpile of enriched uranium was not going anywhere

stories.title: Trump says U.S. blockade of Iranian ports will ‘remain’ if no deal reached
stories.description: Mr. Trump told reporters there were "not going to be tolls" imposed by Iran on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, something that the Islamic republic put forward during previous peace deal plans.

stories.title: Temporary truce: On U.S.-Israel ceasefire with Iran
stories.description: The U.S.-Israel ceasefire with Iran must hold across all fronts

stories.title: Iran-Israel war highlights: Iran reopens Strait of Hormuz; Trump says will bring Iran uranium 'back home to the USA', and more
stories.description: Iran announces reopening of Strait of Hormuz for commercial vessels for the remainder of the two-week U.S.-Iran truce

stories.title: Watch: Constitution Amendment Bill, part of delimitation package, defeated
stories.description: Major political and geopolitical developments lead today’s headlines. The Constitution Amendment Bill on women’s quota faces a setback in the Lok Sabha, while Sri Lanka’s Power and Energy Minister resigns amid controversy. Iran has reopened the Strait of Hormuz, easing concerns o...
